zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Booth於1378年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林時，他的父親John Boothe of Barton, Esq.25歲，母親Lady Joanna Trafford20歲。他於1436年與Isabell Carrington在英國藍卡爾巴頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1460年9月14日在英國藍卡爾布拉可洛德享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas Booth于1378年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林时，他的父亲John Boothe of Barton, Esq.25岁，母亲Lady Joanna Trafford20岁。他于1436年与Isabell Carrington在英国藍卡爾巴頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于1460年9月14日在英国藍卡爾布拉可洛德享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Booth was born in 1378, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., was 25 and his mother, Lady Joanna Trafford, was 20. He married Isabell Carrington in 1436, in Barton, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died on 14 September 1460, in Blackrod, Lancashire, England, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Booth est né en 1378, à Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Joanna Trafford, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Isabell Carrington en 1436, à Barton, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé le 14 septembre 1460, à Blackrod, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas Booth 1378 in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Boothe of Barton, Esq. 25 und seine Mutter Lady Joanna Trafford 20. Er hat 1436 in Barton, Lancashire, England Isabell Carrington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb am 14. September 1460 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Blackrod, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Booth è nato il nel 1378 nel Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Lady Joanna Trafford, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Isabell Carrington nel 1436, nel Barton, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì il 14 settembre 1460, nel Blackrod, Lancashire, England, all'età di 82 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Booth(이)가 1378년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Boothe of Barton, Esq.(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Joanna Trafford(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1436년 영국 랭카실르 바턴에서 Isabell Carrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1460년 9월 14일 영국 랭카실르 블랙로드에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Booth nasceu em 1378, em Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joanna Trafford, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabell Carrington em 1436, em Barton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1460, em Blackrod, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 82 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas Booth родился в 1378 г. в местности аштон-ундир-лин, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Boothe of Barton, Esq. было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joanna Trafford, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabell Carrington в 1436 г. в местности бартон, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер 14 сентября 1460 г. в местности блэккрод, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas Booth nació en 1378, en Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Joanna Trafford, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Isabell Carrington en 1436, en Barton, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1460, en Blackrod, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 82 años.
